data.title: Costco's Quarterly Profit Surpasses Expectations Amid Continued Sales Growth
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corp. reported a higher-than-anticipated increase in quarterly profit, reflecting the company's ability to leverage ongoing sales growth. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the retail giant's performance exceeded market forecasts, driven by strong consumer demand and effective cost management strategies.

The company's financial results underscore its resilience in a competitive retail environment, where maintaining customer loyalty and managing supply chain challenges are crucial. Costco's membership-based model continues to attract a steady stream of shoppers, contributing to its robust sales figures.

Analysts note that Costco's strategic focus on value offerings and efficient operations has positioned it well to navigate economic fluctuations. The retailer's ability to sustain growth momentum is seen as a positive indicator for its future performance.

Overall, Costco's latest earnings report reinforces its status as a leading player in the retail sector, with a strong foundation for continued success.

data.title: Netflix Imposes Restrictions on Podcast Clip Distribution
data.description: Netflix has introduced new limitations for podcasters regarding the distribution of clips from their shows. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that podcasters are now restricted in the number of clips they can share on external platforms. This move is part of Netflix's strategy to maintain control over its content and ensure exclusivity. The restrictions aim to prevent the widespread dissemination of podcast content outside Netflix's ecosystem, potentially impacting the promotional strategies of podcasters who rely on sharing clips to attract listeners. The decision reflects Netflix's broader approach to content management and distribution, as the company continues to navigate the evolving media landscape.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Urges Iranian Diplomats to Foster a Better Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has called on Iranian diplomats to assist in shaping a more prosperous Iran. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the importance of diplomatic efforts in improving relations and fostering positive changes within Iran. This appeal comes amid ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran, highlighting the need for dialogue and cooperation. Trump's statement reflects a strategic approach to international diplomacy, aiming to address longstanding issues and promote stability in the region.

data.title: SEC Finalizes Settlement with Tron Founder Justin Sun Over Fraud Case
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has concluded a settlement with Tron founder Justin Sun and his company over allegations of fraud and unregistered securities. According to NS3.AI, the SEC initially filed a lawsuit against Sun and Tron in March 2023. The case saw a temporary suspension in February of the previous year due to a change in the SEC's leadership. The resolution marks the end of the legal proceedings initiated by the agency.

data.title: Foreign Central Banks Reduce U.S. Treasury Holdings by $9.366 Billion
data.description: Foreign central banks decreased their holdings of U.S. Treasuries by $9.366 billion for the week ending March 6. According to Jin10, this reduction follows a previous decline of $15.061 billion. The data indicates a continued trend of foreign central banks adjusting their U.S. Treasury portfolios. The reasons behind these adjustments remain a subject of analysis among financial experts, as they could reflect broader economic strategies or responses to global market conditions.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stock Markets Decline Amid Ongoing Middle East Conflict
data.description: European stock markets closed with significant losses as the conflict in the Middle East continues. According to RTHK, Germany's DAX index fell by 389 points, or 1.6%, to 23,815 points. France's CAC index decreased by 121 points, approximately 1.5%, ending at 8,045 points. Meanwhile, the UK's FTSE 100 index dropped 153 points, about 1.5%, closing at 10,413 points.

data.title: Dollar Index Rises Amid Currency Exchange Rate Fluctuations
data.description: The dollar index, which measures the U.S. dollar against six major currencies, increased by 0.56% to close at 99.316. According to ChainCatcher, the exchange rate for the euro fell to 1.1583 dollars from the previous day's 1.1625 dollars. The British pound was exchanged at 1.3328 dollars, down from 1.3351 dollars. Meanwhile, the dollar strengthened against the Japanese yen, reaching 157.77 yen compared to 157.15 yen the day before. The Swiss franc saw a slight increase, with the dollar trading at 0.7827 francs, up from 0.78 francs. The Canadian dollar also experienced a rise, with the exchange rate moving to 1.3697 dollars from 1.3669 dollars. Additionally, the Swedish krona saw the dollar exchange rate increase to 9.2657 krona from 9.1761 krona.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Advocates for Bold Development in Ethereum Community
data.description: Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin has called on the community to embrace a more daring and open-minded approach to development. According to NS3.AI, Buterin emphasized the importance of maintaining core principles such as censorship resistance, privacy, and security. He encouraged developers to challenge non-core assumptions by engaging in thought experiments, such as rewriting the application section of the 2014 whitepaper and considering what projects to prioritize if Ethereum had no users today.

data.title: Gap's Fourth-Quarter Results Fall Short of Expectations
data.description: Gap Inc. announced its fourth-quarter financial results, revealing a performance that did not meet market expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that the company's Old Navy and Athleta brands were the primary contributors to the underperformance. Despite efforts to boost sales, these brands struggled to achieve anticipated growth, impacting the overall financial outcome for the quarter. Gap's management is now focusing on strategies to enhance brand performance and drive future growth.

data.title: Culper Research Bets Against Ethereum and BitMine Amid Concerns Over 'Fusaka' Upgrade
data.description: Short seller Culper Research has released a report expressing skepticism about ether (ETH) and BitMine (BMNR). According to NS3.AI, the report argues that Ethereum's 'Fusaka' upgrade in December 2025 has significantly reduced fees by approximately 90%, negatively impacting ETH tokenomics and undermining the bullish network metrics promoted by BitMine chairman Tom Lee. Culper Research suggests that the reduction in fees is compressing staking yields, which could potentially lead to a 'death spiral' characterized by decreased validator demand and weakened network security. Additionally, the report highlights that Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has sold nearly 20,000 ETH. It also estimates that BitMine's treasury of 4.4 million ETH is about 45% underwater, with unrealized losses amounting to approximately $7.4 billion.

data.title: FBI Detects Suspicious Activity on Computer Systems
data.description: The FBI announced on Thursday that it has identified suspicious activity within its computer systems. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that the agency did not disclose additional information regarding the nature or extent of the activity. The investigation into the matter is ongoing, and the FBI is working to determine the source and impact of the activity. Further updates are expected as the situation develops.

data.title: Launch of Bitaxe Turbo Touch: A New Home Bitcoin Miner
data.description:
Solo Satoshi has introduced the Bitaxe Turbo Touch, an open-source touchscreen bitcoin miner designed for home users. According to NS3.AI, this compact device offers a mining performance of approximately 2.15 terahashes per second (TH/s). It utilizes dual BM1370 ASIC chips, achieving an efficiency of around 18 joules per terahash (J/TH) while consuming about 43 watts of power and generating noise levels of approximately 35 decibels.

Solo Satoshi has made the firmware and hardware designs of the Bitaxe Turbo Touch available under an open hardware license. This positions the device as a cost-effective alternative to Braiins' BMM 101, offering a lower cost per terahash for users interested in home-based bitcoin mining.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | CME Lowers Initial Margin for COMEX 5000 Silver Futures
data.description: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) has announced a reduction in the initial margin requirement for its COMEX 5000 silver futures. According to Jin10, the margin has been decreased from 18% to 14%. This adjustment is expected to impact traders and investors dealing in silver futures, potentially influencing market dynamics. The change reflects CME's ongoing assessment of market conditions and risk management strategies. The decision may also affect trading volumes and liquidity in the silver futures market.

data.title: Cambodia Faces International Pressure to Address Scam Trade
data.description: Cambodia is under significant international scrutiny to tackle a billion-dollar scam trade. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges the country faces in curbing these fraudulent activities. The complexity of the scams makes enforcement difficult, as they often involve sophisticated operations that are hard to dismantle. Authorities are urged to intensify efforts to combat these scams, which have far-reaching impacts on the global economy. Despite the pressure, the path to effectively stopping these operations remains fraught with obstacles.

data.title: U.S. Regulators Clarify Capital Rules for Tokenized Securities
data.description: U.S. banking regulators have issued joint guidance indicating that banks will not be required to hold additional capital for blockchain-based or tokenized securities. According to NS3.AI, the Federal Reserve, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) stated that existing capital rules are technology-neutral, meaning tokenized securities will be treated the same as traditional securities. This clarification was released in response to increasing interest from banks in using tokenization to represent ownership rights in securities. The guidance is timely, as there is growing activity in tokenized shares and tokenized U.S. Treasury products from both crypto firms and major asset managers.

data.title: Senator Mullin Surprised by Call to Replace Noem as Homeland Security Secretary
data.description: Senator Markwayne Mullin expressed surprise at receiving a call from U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday regarding his potential appointment as Homeland Security Secretary. Bloomberg posted on X that Mullin was considered to replace Kristi Noem, who he acknowledged had done her best under challenging circumstances. The unexpected call highlights ongoing changes within the administration.

data.title: Iran Conflict Highlights Limitations of Advanced Air Defense Systems
data.description: The ongoing conflict with Iran has highlighted the challenges faced by high-end air defense systems in countering continuous attacks from more affordable enemy missiles, drones, and guided bombs. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the need for a reassessment of current defense strategies. The chief strategy officer of Anduril, a defense technology company, pointed out that these advanced systems are struggling to effectively manage the persistent threat posed by these less expensive yet effective weapons. This situation calls for a strategic evaluation to enhance defense capabilities and address the vulnerabilities exposed by the conflict.
